mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings upward and pushing S&P 500 prices higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE drops below 6.0%, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and lifts equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during confirmed tightening episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit margins and forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat despite GDP growth dropping below 1.5%, or the signal yields a negative Rank IC over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coupled with surging consumer credit balances indicates robust household spending power, which drives earnings revisions upward and lifts S&P 500 prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive forward returns in more than 55% of test periods, or the cross-product term shows no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which lowers unit costs and supports higher equity valuations across all market conditions over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5% year-over-year, inflation expectations anchor higher, prompting restrictive policy that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite CPI inflation exceeding 2.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing.
